name: One-skillet: Spicy Vegetable Noodle Stir Fry
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 0 minutes
Tofu may be regular, firm or extra-firm and should be drained prior to use. Chili oil is usually found in the Chinese food section of grocery stores. Liquid red pepper sauce (Durkee Red Hot, Tabasco, etc.) may be used instead of chili sauce.
1. Place tofu cubes on a double layer of paper towels; let drain for 20 minutes while you prepare other ingredients.
2. Whisk together 1/2 cup broth and cornstarch in a medium bowl. Mix in soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, and crushed red pepper; set aside.
3. Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a wide non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat (depending on your pan). Add tofu cubes and stir-fry until golden, about 4 minutes. Transfer tofu to a plate using a slotted spoon.
4. Add another tbsp of oil to the skillet and set over high heat. Chop or slice mushrooms. Add mushrooms and red pepper to the pan; stir-fry until mushrooms are just tender. Add ginger, garlic, and chili pepper; stir-fry briefly. Remove to tofu platter.
5. Add broccoli florets to the skillet and 1/4 cup chicken broth. Let bubble until broccoli just turns bright green.
6. Whisk together the broth-cornstarch mixture and add it to the broccoli. Bring to a boil. Dump in the reserved tofu mixture and stir-fry, adding more chicken broth if needed to coat evenly. Taste and season with more sesame oil, hot chili oil, and/or dried red chili peppers.
Serve over noodles or rice.
